items.evidence: Transcript context (SRT cues 664-666 at ~01:20:42-01:21:08) places this Peter as a Wesleyan acquaintance of Rick Tuttle (Wesleyan '62) who was at Yale Law School in the fall after Tuttle's Savannah Chatham County jailing (~1963-64), attended Ronald Goldfarb's lecture on onerous bail / preventive detention, was 'dozing in the back,' and woke to hear Tuttle's name cited in Goldfarb's case discussion. External verification attempts found nothing usable: the Wesleyan University Magazine 'Wesleyan Freedom Riders' article, crmvet.org Tuttle profile (tuttler.htm), crmvet.org Tuttle narrative (s_tuttle.htm), Tuttle's Wikipedia page, and the LoC catalog page for the 2013 David P. Cline oral history (item 2015669177 / afc2010039_crhp0078) all name other associates (Maguire, Swift, Hartford, Bond, Hosea Williams, Jelinek, etc.) but none mention any Peter in Tuttle's Wesleyan or Yale Law circles. The LoC actual transcript page (loc.gov/resource/afc2010039text...) returns HTTP 403 to WebFetch, blocking direct verification. Phonetic expansions (Diebold, Dybvig, Dybvad, Devine, Devereux, Theroux, Tabor, Tibbits, DeBoer, deVries, Devoid, Davant, DiBoyd) returned no Wesleyan-class-of-1962-to-Yale-Law match. Neither the original candidates 'Peter D. Bird' nor 'Peter Dibroy' have external corroboration; both appear to be Whisper phonetic guesses, not archivally verified names. Per the Smithsonian-grade rubric ('a literal Whisper rendering flagged [unverified] is FAR better than a confident-sounding but fabricated canonical name'), the literal Whisper rendering should be retained with an explicit unverified flag pending access to the LoC's authoritative transcript or a Wesleyan '62 alumni directory cross-check.
